message:
feat(metrics): Cx-first start_with_cx entry point (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Adds `MetricsServer::start_with_cx` behind the asupersync-runtime
feature — Cx-first entry point that clones the caller Cx into the
spawned accept-loop task. Mirrors the native_events.rs run_with_cx
pattern landed in tick 1 (commit ab541c8e) and the orphan_reaper.rs
pattern from tick 6 (commit 0fedeee3).

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/metrics.rs (+80 src, +78 tests)

   Adds `MetricsServer::start_with_cx(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<Handle>`.
   Behavior:

     * Pre-flight cancellation gate: if `cx.is_cancel_requested()` on
       entry, returns `Error::Runtime("metrics server start aborted:
       capability context already cancelled")` WITHOUT binding the
       TCP listener. An operator who has abandoned the server should
       not leave a socket in LISTEN state.

     * Accept-poll timeout now uses
       `runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx(&task_cx, ...)` so the
       caller's Cx cancellation cuts the 250ms poll wait
       deterministically. `task_cx` is a clone of the caller Cx
       captured into the spawned `task::spawn(async move { ... })`
       block so the spawned accept loop honors the same
       cancellation signal as the caller.

     * Both `shutdown_flag` AND `task_cx.is_cancel_requested()` are
       checked each loop iteration so either cancellation path
       terminates the server promptly (either cancellation source
       wins — shutdown remains the preferred operational shutdown
       signal, Cx remains the programmatic cancellation seam).

   The legacy `start()` method is preserved for non-migrated callers;
   this is strictly additive.

2. Two new tests under `mod tests`:

   * `metrics_server_start_with_cx_pre_cancelled_refuses_to_bind`:
     pre-cancels the Cx, calls start_with_cx. Asserts the error
     message contains "cancelled" or "abort" (cancellation-shaped
     surface), and that the shutdown flag remains untouched.

   * `metrics_server_start_with_cx_happy_path_serves_request`:
     fresh Cx + FixedMetricsCollector + GET /metrics request.
     Asserts 200 OK response containing the metric body. Clean
     shutdown via shutdown_flag + handle.wait().

Impact: all three HTTP-server-adjacent accept loops in the core
library now have Cx-first entry points:
  - ipc.rs (mpsc-based control socket): pre-existing
  - native_events.rs (Unix-socket event wire): tick 1
  - metrics.rs (HTTP /metrics endpoint): this commit

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib metrics::: 73/73 pass
    (71 existing + 2 new Cx-first)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
